item.title: Finally
item.content:encoded:
I love this one. It's clever, it's creepy and weird, and, most importantly, it's not a grimdark hyper-malevolent entity. It's weird at first and then jumps to frightening without ever needing to go into gruesome details or excessive interview logs.

+1

Also, thanks for including transcripts. Since I read most SCPs in public or at work, I don't always have the ability to listen to the audio on these things. I kinda think all audio recordings on this site need to have a transcription, even if it's not in the main article for continuity reasons.

item.title: Re: Made a scketch about this SCP
item.content:encoded: Thanks, it's honestly one of my favorite SCPs, so creative, one of the best format screws and a great example of why the foundation dosen't test with things a lot

item.title: Re: Made a scketch about this SCP
item.content:encoded: As a former theatre student myself, I am thrilled that you used my piece for one of your classes. Even though I couldn't understand the language, I could tell when you switched characters so that's a good sign.

item.title: Made a scketch about this SCP
item.content:encoded:
On my theater class we had to create a video of suspense, and using outside storyes was alowed, so I decided to use 3086 as a base. Thought I should let you know: https://youtu.be/-HeDERXjcpE

Be wary that it's in portuguese and I don't have plans of adding english subtitles in the near future

item.title: Re: Copyright
item.content:encoded: Roger that! Added to Author Post.

item.title: Copyright
item.content:encoded:
Although I'm reasonably confident that this excerpt from one of Stephen R. Donaldson's "Thomas Covenant" novels is brief enough to count as fair use, we should probably credit him somewhere.

(I would also say that the word "graveling" was most likely intended to be "grave - ling" - i.e., some kind of zombie - and not "gravel - ing".)

item.title: About having the scp in text form
item.content:encoded:
Have you considered doing a version of what they did in 4000 Taboo and have the adapted page be a separate one? Maybe even in Disscuss. This might help maintain the immersion wile still providing people with a more practical altenative

You could even create a in-universe explanation, maybe a researcher has hearing problems so they create temporary pages with no links containing the info for him
What can I say? I’m proud of my little Chinchilla that could.

item.title: Re: Potential Error
item.content:encoded: Excellent catch, friend! Thank you!

item.title: Potential Error
item.content:encoded:
"before arriving the in the email address of a Nintendo of America employee"

Did you mean to say before arriving in the?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe Hyde it? Or in order to access it you have to go to a "secure" computer or website, such as one that, in universe, isn't conected to the internet, or one without external links (I'm not shure it is possible to do this on the wiki, but would be worth a try)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this meant to be a parody of narcissistic douchey webcomic artists? if it is, +1
Here’s to George spreading the Gospel of Saul Szyslak far and wide!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Instances can be created by writing or typing its proper name, “George the Chinchilla”

I upvoted at this point and nothing after that made me reconsider.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I did! Great work, esp. at the end of Log 2! :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On Russell's end, I totally get the "staying true to the art" and in-universe reasons for getting rid of the text…

But on the opposite side, I've got
"Hearing Impaired Fans can read it."
"People on a bus with no headphones can read it."
"It takes 11 minutes to listen to but 2-5 minutes to read."

and at the end of the day, I am nothing if not practical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The reasons for the text is due to people who can't listen to the audio file for whatever reason.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: YOu know me, ima fan. and I gotta say…. delete the texts and let it be audio only…. their are others like this here… especially in the memetics division. Don't be scared about it being an audio only file…. in the memetic department…. their are layers…. upon layers… you got my upvote but I do concur with others… kill the text,,, let it be audio only. that just my humble opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the way you're thinking about it! With the way the anomaly manifests, I (and others) have already spitballed writing prompts for Tales that would amazing. Some of my favorites:

Fred vs George (Obviously)
George vs Murphy Law
A young boy who's only friend is his imaginary friend George who lives in his journal.

Also, posted the audio version! I hope you enjoy it!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In glad you like it! Also, to add a little terror to the finale, have you ever seen that episode of Doctor Who where looking at a Weeping Angel for long enough imprints the angel in your mind so it was literally attacking from inside her eye?

That's my headcanon for what's happening in the finale. Creeps me the heck out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
and no transcription is to be made of the audio post-recording.

[BEGIN LOG]

I honestly thought there was going to be more to that, as I'm specifically reading what should apparently never ever be done, so I was waiting for that shoe to drop as to why there's a log, when I just read Don't Write It Down OH GOD DON'T DO IT. This is the kind of article that should just be all audio, unless the switch to the written log was a key part of the narrative. To me, there's too much of a disconnect between what the anomaly is and how it's actually presented, since that's part and parcel of it. Still, +1 because the l liked the excerpts of infected works, and that you said you hope to change it to full audio possibly.

I hope that made some amount of sense ^_^, and that I didn't completely misread the entry in the first place :P

edit: I was also thinking that 3086 wasn't going to actually be this thing at all, but a completely unrelated skip whose entry was in the process of being infected by George.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is really good. Well-written, and no longer than it needs to be. I predicted the final addendum getting infected as soon as I started reading it, but this is good regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks, I had not seen SCP-1159 before. It actually strengthens my reasons for presenting it all via text and letting the reader suspend disbelief; I'm on my phone right now and I can't view Flash, so I can't listen to 1159.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's really good actually, I will be disappointed if other people don't enjoy it as much.

Kinda reminds me of that harp SCP that would cause a incident if anything was written about it so it needed to be described in an audio file which is SCP-1159, I think it's different enough to not be like that one though.

Also the twist of the guy getting his head shot causing the guy to spread to minds was amazingly dark. Even if everyone else doesn't like it and it gets deleted, I recommend you try again, you are really good at writing SCPs.

TL;DR: haha it's lit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is precedent for that sort of thing. SCP-1159, for instance.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm slightly disappointed you didn't add in the mention of a GK-Class End-Of-Narrative-Cohesion Scenario, but nonetheless, I enjoyed this! This was fun criting on the SCPD Discord!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You actually nailed it on the head when you mentioned Fred: this effects the story on the page rather than the mind. Or at least, it's only the page until the final test makes it mad and gives it a power boost.

Audio Log 1: Hey, we've got this dopey anomaly, lets poke it with a stick.
Audio Log 2: Nope, we should not have done that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah I'm confused in that aspect, since memetic stuff usually involves messing with people's mind. So this is more like "Fred" that it exist within narratives instead of minds.

But then the second log came, which says the entire site is infected and quarantined, which made me think that the person who recorded the second log rather than the audio log itself that got infected.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
lol I 100% get that! I typically do a lot of "Audio Adaptations" of SCPs, and this is my concept for "SCP that has to be recorded because text spreads the anomaly."

My endgame is to record it because Audio is the "real" canon format, but I thought it had to be in text for the wiki. (And I wanted the SCP to stand on its own before I recorded it.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah! I see what you mean:

In the beginning, "people" were not infectable. Books, websites, and pictures would change, but if a person mentioned George out loud, it wouldn't matter at all; it only mattered if they wrote or drew him.

It was only after Test SCP-3086-F that a person talking about George became an infection vector. The Foundation thought they had a solid grasp on the anomaly, but that test levelled it up in an unexpected way.

If there's a way that "people" being infectable earlier in the script seemed likely, let me know. I'd love to clean it up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But wouldn't the reader i.e. the person who first read and record the file be infected? Like, images and text are the vector, but they have to be read by someone for that person to be infected. Thus having someone reading aloud the text while recording it would expose them to the anomaly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea is good but the whole "this is not allowed to be transcribed" part of having an article that's technically comprised of transcribed audio just feels a bit ironic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In defense of the arguments presented:

It had been extensively tested that only text and images transmitted the anomaly; no speech or audio recording had never spread the anomaly until the second audio log(Post Test F). So reading from an affected text had never been a problem until the finale.

I hope that clarifies a little; is there a way I could make that clearer in the earlier parts of the article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The excerpt in the first audio log actually breaks the immersion for me—sure, it's competently written, but for someone to read aloud a sample of known info/cognito hazard? I found it really hard to believe. And wouldn't it considered a breach if it's read from a written object? Or if it's purely from memory, I feel it'd be really redundant for the memorizer of the document to remember a sample of the anomaly, especially one that's that long.

And we do know that information tend to mutate within a telephone game, which this document basically is, so the Foundation should've at least speculated that the second Audio Log could happen.

Novoting.

item.title: Author post?
item.content:encoded:
Excerpt of novel used is from Lord Foul’s Bane, first book in The Chronicles of Thomas Covenant, the Unbeliever by Stephen R. Donaldson.

Major Update: Audio Format has been uploaded! Leaving the text versions for people who can't listen and are willing to suspend disbelief.

Oh god, and I was just a recording guy before…

Super thanks to
Ba55
Yossipossi
Modulum
for speccing this before posting in the forums and on Discord.

Is the audio fighting you? Here's a YouTube link:
https://youtu.be/Dg-9H6WuRBA

+ "Why is this text if the whole point of the SCP in-universe is that it HAS to be audio format only?"
- Hope this makes sense

If you've seen me around the site, it's because I have recorded Audio Adaptations of dozens of SCPs. When I came up with this concept, I realized two things about it that I wanted to make sure didn't happen.

1: I didn't want my first SCP to only be good based on the audio gimmick alone, so having it be "Audio Logs" as we are used to on site is a good way to make sure the writing holds up and let suspension of disbelief that these are recorded do its job for now.

2: Letting the story go on the wiki text first allowed me to make any edits if anybody happened to spot something I didn't see or if the community came up with something that improves George vastly. It's far easier to edit a text than an audio post.

Now that the Audio Version (The True Canon) is complete, I'm leaving the logs up for people who can't listen to it because of being in public or because it's eleven minutes long.

and

+ Some Themes present in this SCiP
- Or at least I think so

A: What if Fred was malevolent and had SCP-3043's powers?

B:
Audio Log 1: Hey, we have this dopey anomaly, let's poke it with a stick.

Audio Log 2: Nope, we should not have done that.

C: What if a fictional character just wanted people to care about it's creator?

A Tumblr Post of SCP-3086 Fan Art and that’s very exciting.

Check out more from me over at TheeSherm’s Author Page and Site-42 Hub
